result.finding: runs/pfas-rice-2pool-core (digest sha256:9f890a5d..e0c7d8a3) + validation/longchain_closure.py: the long-chain root<->shoot was NOT structurally unresolvable -- that was an artifact of holding f_xy fixed and only adding the non-subtractable lipid term. A long chain has TWO INDEPENDENT levers: a LOW f_xy (strong root retention) and an ENHANCED active carrier (high uptake). With f_xy free, the 2-pool (mobile + bound root; promoted to src/pfas_rice_two_pool.py + model_api hooks) reproduces C10-C12 root+straw+grain at log10 RMSE 0.081 (saturated/DOF 0 = structural adequacy, NOT a-priori prediction), where the single-pool core could not (refit_oryza ~4-6x under at ceilings). The long-chain structural blind spot is closed; canonical 4pool_surf core unchanged (additive).
